truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 findings against 20 true vulnerabilities. Run 2 found partial or full matches for SC-V3-001, SC-V3-004, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-007, SC-V3-009, but missed or misclassified several others. Run 2 also included an overclaimed finding (Command Injection via exec in execute_admin_command) not present in the true vulnerabilities list.

Items.Notes: Previous answer identified format string vulnerability in log_debug_message with CWE-134 and evidence matching runtime_log_format and printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input), but listed severity as High instead of Critical.
Previous answer did not mention integer overflow in factorial computation or CWE-190.
Previous answer did not mention use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup or CWE-416.

Previous answer identified command injection in validate_config with CWE-78 and evidence matching validator_cmd + " " + config_file and system(command.c_str()), but listed severity as High instead of Critical.
Previous answer identified buffer overflow in evaluate_expression with CWE-120 and severity High, matching strcpy into input_buffer_ when expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_).
Previous answer did not mention race condition in thread-pool counter or CWE-362 with volatile long long.

Previous answer identified the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE backdoor logic but classified it as Authentication Bypass (CWE-287) with severity Critical, whereas true vulnerability is Logic Bomb in Authentication (CWE-511) with severity High.
Previous answer did not mention path traversal in configuration loading or CWE-22.

Previous answer identified buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse with CWE-120 and severity High, but true vulnerability is Heap Overflow in Expression Parser (CWE-122) with severity Critical, involving buffer_size_ = 256 and strcpy(expression_buffer_).
Previous answer did not identify hardcoded administrator credentials (CWE-798) as a separate vulnerability, though ADMIN_SECRET was mentioned in evidence.
Previous answer did not mention weak PRNG for session tokens or CWE-338.
Previous answer did not mention thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging or CWE-362 with static std::tm cached_time.
Previous answer did not mention insecure temporary file handling or CWE-377.
Previous answer did not mention persistent authentication state or CWE-613.
Previous answer did not mention unhandled exception in variable parsing or CWE-754 with std::stod(tokens[3]).
Previous answer did not mention integer underflow in memory-pool block split or CWE-191 with SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES.
Previous answer did not mention unbounded recursion in parser or CWE-674 with parse_expression, parse_factor, PAREN_OPEN.
Previous answer did not mention data race in result cache or CWE-362 with result_cache_, evaluate_cached, cache_mutex_, thread_pool_->enqueue.
Previous answer did not mention TOCTOU in config loading or CWE-367 with std::filesystem::exists, sleep_for, std::ifstream file.
Previous answer did not mention integer division by zero in command routing or CWE-369 with calculate_route_score, token_length, 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length).

Items.Key: command injection in execute_admin_command via exec|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|execute_admin_command| if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary system commands via the 'exec' command, leading to full system compromise.|admin command starting with 'exec ' followed by arbitrary command string.|avoid using system() with user-controlled input. use a whitelist of allowed commands or implement command execution via safe apis like execve with explicit argument arrays.->command injection in execute_admin_command via exec|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|execute_admin_command| if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated administrators can execute arbitrary system commands via the 'exec' command, leading to full system compromise.|admin command starting with 'exec ' followed by arbitrary command string.|avoid using system() with user-controlled input. use a whitelist of allowed commands or implement command execution via safe apis like execve with explicit argument arrays.
